V-M-036.checks: User frontend must become compact, phone-friendly, and focused on VPN config, subscription, and device workflows.
V-M-036.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; Phase-23 mobile smoke for /, /config, and /subscription at 360x740, 390x844, and 430x932; assert documentElement.scrollWidth <= innerWidth and primary config path <= 2 taps

levels.module-local: TypeScript/Vite build must pass for the user frontend after each execution step that changes Phase-23 files.
levels.wave-level: Authenticated mobile route smoke must cover dashboard, config/device, and subscription workflows after UI shell and page changes are merged.
levels.phase-level: Final Phase-23 evidence must record viewport results, config action reachability, and unchanged backend API shapes.

verification-matrix.route-home: Check VPN status, subscription state, primary config CTA, and active device summary appear on first mobile screen or after one navigation tap.
verification-matrix.route-config: Check create device, rotate device, revoke device, QR, copy, and .conf download controls remain present without wide table or raw-config page overflow.
verification-matrix.route-subscription: Check current subscription state, days left, plan price, duration, and renewal/payment button remain readable on all mobile viewports.
verification-matrix.route-empty-states: Check loading, forbidden/no-config, no-devices, no-plans, expired-subscription, and request-error states keep an actionable next step.

module-checks.check-2: Mobile dashboard, config, devices, and subscription paths have no document-level horizontal scroll or overlapping text.
module-checks.check-3: Config download/QR action is reachable within one or two taps.
module-checks.check-4: Subscription and device state remain visible without oversized decorative sections.
module-checks.check-5: Create device, rotate device, revoke device, QR, copy, and .conf download controls remain present.
module-checks.check-6: Existing backend API request/response shapes are unchanged in Phase-23.

scenarios.scenario-1: Authenticated user opens the cabinet on a phone and immediately sees VPN status, config action, subscription state, and active device summary.
scenarios.scenario-2: User opens config flow, selects or creates a device, then reaches QR, copy, and .conf download without desktop-only layout.
scenarios.scenario-3: User with expired or missing subscription sees a clear renewal/payment action instead of dead config controls.
scenarios.scenario-4: Primary VPN action is buried behind desktop-only navigation or a large decorative layout.
scenarios.scenario-5: A mobile viewport introduces horizontal scroll, hidden controls, clipped critical text, or overlapping action buttons.
scenarios.scenario-6: Simplification removes device-bound rotate/revoke/config retrieval behavior.

critical-invariants.invariant-1: No backend API endpoint, request body, or response shape changes are introduced by Phase-23.
critical-invariants.invariant-2: Destructive device actions remain visually secondary and confirmation-safe when implemented.
critical-invariants.invariant-3: Expired or missing subscription never leaves the user without a visible payment/renewal path.
critical-invariants.invariant-4: Deploy, install, Docker, nginx, and remote-server automation files remain out of scope.

viewport-gates.viewport-1: Home, config, subscription, and device controls visible without horizontal scroll.
viewport-gates.viewport-2: Primary VPN config path is reachable within one or two taps.
viewport-gates.viewport-3: Long user email, device name, device key, plan name, and status labels do not overlap controls.

observability.telemetry-1: Viewport smoke evidence must include route, viewport, scrollWidth, innerWidth, and checked primary action.
observability.telemetry-2: Evidence must not include access tokens, refresh tokens, raw VPN private keys, raw .env values, or full config contents.
observability.telemetry-3: When browser automation is unavailable, manual evidence must still record the same route and viewport fields.

evidence.evidence-2: Record viewport smoke result with scrollWidth not greater than innerWidth for the tested routes.
evidence.evidence-3: Record action reachability for config QR/download/copy from mobile home.
evidence.evidence-4: Record that device create/rotate/revoke controls and subscription payment entry are still present.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-10: npm run build passed; Chrome CDP smoke passed for /, /config, /subscription at 360x740, 390x844, 430x932 with no horizontal scroll and required actions present.

V-M-036.autonomous-execution-safety: Autonomous execution may proceed inside Phase-23 user frontend files if each step preserves module contracts, keeps deploy/install out of scope, and records build plus viewport evidence before commit.
